phase: Phase P2P-A Runtime Audit Accepted-Risk Decision Pack
summary: Formal accepted-risk decision pack for the Solana transitive vulnerabilities surfaced by npm audit. This document does NOT change CI policy; npm audit remains a blocking gate. This document does NOT enable any runtime path; all live transfer flags remain disarmed. The pack records the structural reasoning for an operator decision and provides the JSON the policy can later consult if/when the operator signs the companion markdown.

operatorApproval.scope: Phase P2P production canary readiness only; does not authorize public payment or open mainnet transfer
operatorApproval.instruction: Signed by the AHIN operator. Effective until validUntil. The signed-audit-policy gate (scripts/check-runtime-audit-policy.mjs) consults this block. Re-review mandatory on or before validUntil, or earlier on any advisory re-review trigger.

policyEffectIfSigned.description: When this pack is signed (operatorApproval.signedBy populated), a SUBSEQUENT operator-authorized CI-policy commit may consult this file to decide whether to treat specific advisory IDs as accepted vs blocking. That CI policy change is a separate decision recorded as its own commit in a separate phase. THIS commit does not implement any such policy.
policyEffectIfSigned.rootAdvisoryModel: There are exactly TWO root advisories in the Solana dependency tree: bigint-buffer (GHSA-3gc7-fjrx-p6mg) and uuid (GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq). npm audit additionally flags 14 cascade packages that have NO independent advisory — they are reported only because they transitively depend on a package that depends on one of the two roots. Acceptance is keyed on the two root advisory IDs; the entire transitive cascade is accepted by extension. A genuinely new vulnerability would introduce a NEW root advisory ID not in this list, and the signed-policy gate (scripts/check-runtime-audit-policy.mjs) would FAIL.
policyEffectIfSigned.cascadeNote: 14 cascade packages observed at signing (2026-05-28), 16 total npm-audit entries. Cascade membership may shift as the dependency tree changes; the gate does not pin cascade package names — it pins the two root advisory IDs above.
doesNotAccept: Any future advisory not listed above
Any advisory that breaks the AND-of-explicit-env-flags safety model
Any advisory in a package not in the Solana ecosystem cascade

advisories.title: bigint-buffer Vulnerable to Buffer Overflow via toBigIntLE() Function

reachability.fromRootGovernanceUiNote: Root renders <Gatekeeper> -> <AhinGateway/> (R3F scene). Neither path constructs Solana RPC objects or imports @solana/spl-token directly. bigint-buffer is loaded only when src/lib/lifePlusSolana.ts is exercised (via Gatekeeper's verifySolanaPocc).
reachability.withoutCanaryFlagsNote: When gateMode=live, readLifePlusBalanceRaw imports @solana/spl-token transitively pulling bigint-buffer. The vulnerable function toBigIntLE is invoked when reading u64 token amounts. Current production env keeps gateMode=mock so this path is not reachable today.
reachability.canCauseSigningTransferBurnNote: Even if exploited, bigint-buffer cannot bypass the runtime AND-of-env-flags. Signing/transfer require AHIN_PROTOCOL_EXECUTION_ENABLED=true && AHIN_REAL_USAGE_FEE_TRANSFER=true && AHIN_PAYMENT_CANARY_ENABLED=true && wallet allowlisted && amount within cap. These are evaluated at module import via process.env; runtime code cannot mutate process.env to flip them.

advisories.exploitabilityInThisCodebase: Low. The vulnerability triggers on attacker-controlled Buffer inputs to toBigIntLE. Our code only invokes it via connection.getTokenAccountBalance(ata), where `ata` is a deterministic ATA derived from the user's pubkey + the LIFE++ mint. There is no path for the user to supply a malformed Buffer that flows into toBigIntLE.

availableFix.breakDetail: @solana/spl-token@0.1.8 (released ~2021) predates the modern API used in src/lib/transactionSolana.ts. Specifically createTransferCheckedInstruction and getAssociatedTokenAddressSync are not exported in 0.1.8 — the current code would fail to compile.

advisories.whyForceFixRejected: npm audit fix --force would silently downgrade @solana/spl-token to a version where our transfer-instruction builder cannot be constructed. The build would fail at tsc, and if we patched our code to use 0.1.x APIs we'd lose checked-decimal validation in transfers — a security regression. Cure worse than disease.
mitigation: AND-of-explicit-env-flags safety model (release-lint enforced) blocks signing/transfer/burn regardless of dep behavior.
Wallet signing API confined to src/lib/walletAdapters.ts.
Transaction submission APIs confined to src/lib/walletAdapters.ts.
/active-hash simulator + root governance routes are web3-free.
gateMode=mock + cluster=devnet in current env keeps the entire @solana/spl-token execution path cold.
advisories.residualRisk: If gateMode is flipped to live (P3A) without this accepted-risk pack being signed, every Gatekeeper PoCC check would load the vulnerable dep chain. Risk is bounded to memory-safety of the JS runtime; no signing/transfer can be coerced.

review.expiryRationale: 90 days from issue. If upstream has not patched by then, re-evaluate with current Solana ecosystem status.
triggerEarlyReReview: Solana team publishes patched @solana/spl-token without bigint-buffer dependency
New advisory published against bigint-buffer with different exploit profile
Decision to advance to P3B (real foundation-wallet micro-transfer)
Decision to open canary beyond a single allowlisted wallet

advisories.title: uuid: Missing buffer bounds check in v3/v5/v6 when buf is provided

reachability.fromRootGovernanceUiNote: uuid is used by @solana/web3.js internally for request-id correlation. Our code never calls uuid.v3/v5/v6 with a user-supplied `buf` argument.
reachability.withoutCanaryFlagsNote: Loaded any time a Connection is constructed (lifePlusSolana.ts, paymentPreflight.ts, etc.).
reachability.canCauseSigningTransferBurnNote: Bounds-check bypass in uuid cannot influence process.env or the env-derived gate flags.

advisories.exploitabilityInThisCodebase: Very low. The advisory requires the caller to pass a user-controlled `buf` argument to uuid.v3/v5/v6. Our code does not call these functions directly. @solana/web3.js's internal usage uses uuid.v4 (which is not affected by the advisory).

availableFix.breakDetail: @solana/web3.js pins an older uuid range. npm overrides could force uuid@11.1.1+ but the SDK may not be tested against that major. Could introduce subtle runtime incompatibilities in request correlation.

advisories.whyForceFixRejected: Same rationale as bigint-buffer: forced upgrade may destabilize the SDK in ways that aren't caught at build time. Better to wait for upstream coordination.
mitigation: Same structural mitigations as bigint-buffer entry.
Additional: our code never invokes uuid.v3/v5/v6 directly.
advisories.residualRisk: Minimal beyond what bigint-buffer already presents. Same upstream-fix dependency.

review.expiryRationale: Aligned with bigint-buffer review; the two are practically coupled.
triggerEarlyReReview: Discovery of a uuid.v4 advisory that would affect our code path

advisories.title: Cascading via bigint-buffer + @solana/web3.js
advisories.whyForceFixRejected: Cascade of bigint-buffer fix — same reason.

advisories.title: Cascading via @solana/buffer-layout-utils + @solana/spl-token-group + @solana/spl-token-metadata + @solana/web3.js
advisories.exploitabilityInThisCodebase: Identical to bigint-buffer root cause; surfaced because direct import.
advisories.whyForceFixRejected: Cascade of bigint-buffer fix — same reason.

structuralMitigationsSummary: Wallet signing API confined to src/lib/walletAdapters.ts (release-lint enforced).
Transaction submission APIs confined to src/lib/walletAdapters.ts (release-lint enforced).
Burn instruction path forbidden everywhere (release-lint enforced).
/active-hash simulator + root governance routes are web3-free (release-lint enforced).
Dev routes /api/dev/* return 404 in production (release-lint enforced).
grantAccess fires only after confirmation outcome (release-lint enforced).
Canary allowlist defaults empty; cap defaults 0n (blocks); both env-derived (release-lint enforced).
doNotInThisPatch: Enable public payment, transfer, burn, or signing.
blockers: operatorApproval.signedBy unset — pack is not yet effective.
Even after signing, separate CI-policy commit required to make audit advisory-only-warn for the listed IDs.
